steps.title: Identify the relationship between 'novel' and the title
steps.content: The phrase is “her ______ Gothic (2020).” The title Mexican Gothic is a restrictive appositive — it specifies exactly which novel is meant. Restrictive appositives are not set off by punctuation; they attach directly to the noun they identify.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — novel Mexican: No punctuation between “novel” and “Mexican” — correct for a restrictive appositive. The full phrase reads “her novel Mexican Gothic” without any interruption. ✓
  • Choice B — novel: Mexican: A colon here would imply that what follows is a definition or list, not a title functioning as a restrictive appositive. ✗
  • Choice C — novel, Mexican: A comma turns the appositive nonrestrictive, suggesting the title is merely additional information rather than identifying which novel — that misrepresents the structure. ✗
  • Choice D — novel (Mexican: Opening a parenthesis without closing it creates an unclosed parenthetical — grammatically incomplete. ✗
That matches Choice A — novel Mexican.
